caption: 2018 revenue mix and the three-pillar moat framing (deep liquidity / platform / clearing) — historical baseline with Interest Rates 27%, Energy 18%, Market Data 11% of revenue.
Half-century volume record: 13% pro-forma ADV CAGR 1972-2017, the long-run growth claim management leans on.
Non-US electronic ADV as a share of each asset class in 2018 — the globalization starting point to compare against 2025.
2Q18 adjusted P&L (70.5% operating margin) plus per-asset-class RPC table — historical unit economics.
Dividend-only capital-return model in 2018 (regular + annual variable dividend, no buybacks) — the bookend to the 2025 buyback regime change.
'Compelling Investment' case framing a network effect 'similar to large technology companies' — moat self-description, 2018 vintage.
Updated three-pillar moat framing with the full product map across six asset classes plus OTC, cash Treasuries/repo and crypto.
Q4 2025 ADV by asset class and key highlights: +8% revenue growth, 67.1% adjusted operating margin, fifth consecutive record ADV year.
Growth maturation: 7% ADV / 12% adjusted-net-income CAGR over 2015-2025, and the rising share of trading days above 30M contracts (29% in 2025, 64% 2026TD).
Globalization paying off: non-US ADV 10% CAGR, 60% of front-office sales staff now outside the US (up from 44% in 2018), non-US share by asset class.
Retail base nearly quadrupled in a decade (+23% in 2025); new broker partners Robinhood/eToro/Webull/Plus500 and the December 2025 FanDuel JV.
New-product innovation engine: micros +32% ADV, US credit futures, TBA mortgage futures, and the sustainable (carbon/water/battery-metals) suite.
Interest Rates 2025 revenue of $1.95B decomposed by sub-line (LTIR/STIR futures and options, cleared IRS, BrokerTec); rates ADV doubled 2014 to 2025.
Capital efficiency as the moat's cash value: >$80B/day customer margin savings, split ~40% Equity Index / ~35% Rates, with the cross-margining sources.
CME Securities Clearing (CMESC): a new SEC-regulated clearing house for cash Treasuries and repo launching in 2026 — entry into DTCC/FICC's turf.
RPC mix punch: physical commodities are 20% of F&O ADV but 35% of clearing and transaction-fee revenue; commodities revenue $1,826M, +9% YoY.
Pricing power: OTC-alternative products command 3-4x the standard Equity Index rate per contract; annual ADV roughly tripled 2020 to 2025.
Non-transaction revenue durability: market-data revenue 8% CAGR and S&P DJI equity income 12% CAGR over 2020-2025 — the non-cyclical counterweight.
Google Cloud strategic partnership: 2025 was the final year of incremental net-cash migration cost; 2026 turns net-positive. Aurora region + GCUL ledger.
Pricing philosophy: adjustments add ~1.5-2% to revenue on similar activity (2026 net +1.0-1.5%); month-by-month rolling RPC by asset class.
2025 adjusted income-statement Sankey: $6.521B revenue to $4.525B operating income (69.4% margin) to $4.088B net income — full reconciliation on one page.
Capital-return regime change: opportunistic share repurchases ($3B authorized) added alongside the regular and annual variable dividend; OSTTRA proceeds fund buybacks.
LNG export tailwind for Henry Hub energy volume: US natural-gas exports rising from ~15% of production today to 20-25% by 2030.
Macro volume driver: dispersion in forward Fed-rate expectations historically tracks higher Treasury futures and options volumes.
Management's full 2026 investment case: marketplace position plus growth orientation plus reliable capital return.
